text:
The "replacement fee too low" error happened because:

1. I sent multiple transactions in quick succession (add token → approve → deposit)
2. Each transaction has a "nonce" (sequential number)
3. The second transaction tried to use nonce #7 but with lower gas price than a pending transaction also using nonce #7
4. Base rejected it because you can't replace a transaction with a lower-priced one

Wait for the previous transaction to confirm, or use higher gas price.

This is standard behavior on all EVM chains (Base, Ethereum, etc.) - not specific to Ethereum mainnet.

───

What's Now on GitHub

•

The "replacement fee too low" error happened because:

1. I sent multiple transactions in quick succession (add token → approve → deposit)
2. Each transaction has a "nonce" (sequential number)
3. The second transaction tried to use nonce #7 but with lower gas price than a pending transaction also using nonce #7
4. Base rejected it because you can't replace a transaction with a lower-priced one

Wait for the previous transaction to confirm, or use higher gas price.

This is standard behavior on all EVM chains (Base, Ethereum, etc.) - not specific to Ethereum mainnet.

text.text: For UNAUTHENTICATED trader history (what you need):

# Get ANY wallet's trades (public data - no auth needed)
curl "https://clob.polymarket.com/trades?maker=0xSOME_WALLET_ADDRESS"

# Or use the CLI (already works):
polymarket data trades 0xWALLET_ADDRESS --limit 100 -o json

# Use Gamma API (works unauthenticated):
import requests

# Get top traders by volume on active markets
markets = requests.get("https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets?active=true&limit=10").json()

for market in markets:
    # Get trades on this market
    trades = requests.get(f"https://clob.polymarket.com/trades?market={market['id']}&limit=100").json()
    
    # Aggregate by trader wallet
    trader_volumes = {}
    for trade in trades:
        wallet = trade['maker']
        volume = float(trade['price']) * float(trade['size'])
        trader_volumes[wallet] = trader_volumes.get(wallet, 0) + volume
    
    # Top traders on this market
    top = sorted(trader_volumes.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

").json()

",

text:
--                                                                                                                                                                                                
  AOX Marketplace — Manual Lead Upload Blueprint                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  Overview                                                                                                                                                                                           

  To manually list a lead on the AOX marketplace, you need to edit one file: app/lib/leads.ts in the aox-app repo. You add two things: the lead listing (public info buyers see before purchase) and
  the reveal data (private info unlocked after payment). If revealData exists for a lead ID, the lead is automatically open for sale with all tokens (USDC, USDT, DAI, WETH,

, ETH). No other   
  files need to change.

  ---
  Step 1: Open app/lib/leads.ts

  This file has two sections:
  1. HARDCODED_LEADS array — the public listing cards
  2. revealData object — the private data delivered after purchase

  ---
  Step 2: Add the lead to HARDCODED_LEADS

  Add a new object to the HARDCODED_LEADS array. Place it at the top of the array so it appears first.

  IMPORTANT: The listing must NOT reveal the full details (wallet address, profile link, specific metrics). It should tease the value without giving away what the buyer is paying for.

  Here's the entry for this specific lead:

  {
    id: 'poly-1003',
    category: 'Polymarket',
    title: 'Polymarket Whale — $64M Volume',
    desc: 'Verified high-volume prediction market trader. 500 trades across 56 unique markets. Enterprise-grade intelligence with full wallet and profile data.',
    score: 90,
    price: 100,
    tier: 'enterprise',
    wallet_age: 'Verified',
    liquidity: '$64.2M',
    contacts: 1,
    chain: 'Polygon',
    timestamp: '2026-03-21T05:00:00.000Z',
    win_rate: 'Unverified',
    volume: '$64.2M',
    markets: 56,
  }

  Field rules:
  - id — unique string. For Polymarket leads use poly-XXXX. Increment from the highest existing poly ID (check existing leads).
  - category — must be exactly 'Polymarket' (case-sensitive). This is how the /marketplace/polymarket page filters.
  - title — short, punchy. OK to include volume headline. Do NOT include wallet address.
  - desc — 1-2 sentences selling the value. Do NOT include the wallet, profile URL, or any contact info.
  - score — 0-100 integer.
  - price — number in USDC (e.g., 100 means 100 USDC).
  - tier — one of: 'standard', 'premium', 'enterprise', 'elite'.
  - wallet_age — string like 'Verified', '2+ years', etc.
  - liquidity — string describing volume/TVL.
  - contacts — number of contact points in the reveal data.
  - chain — blockchain name string.
  - timestamp — ISO 8601 date string of when the lead was listed.
  - win_rate — (optional, Polymarket only) string.
  - volume — (optional, Polymarket only) string.
  - markets — (optional, Polymarket only) number.

  ---
  Step 3: Add reveal data to revealData

  In the same file, add an entry to the revealData object using the same id as the key. This is the data the buyer receives after payment.

'poly-1003': {
    fields: [
      { label: 'Wallet Address', value: '0xc2e7800b5af46e6093872b177b7a5e7f0563be51' },
      { label: 'Polymarket Profile', value: '

' },
      { label: 'Total Volume', value: '$64,242,887.69' },
      { label: 'Total Trades', value: '500 (498 buys, 2 sells)' },
      { label: 'Markets Active', value: '56 unique markets' },
      { label: 'Buy Ratio', value: '99.6%' },
      { label: 'Win Rate', value: 'Unverified — cannot be confirmed from trade data alone' },
      { label: 'Chain', value: 'Polygon' },
      { label: 'Data Source', value: 'Polymarket Data API (

)' },
      { label: 'Risk Flags', value: '99.6% buy pattern is abnormal — possible bot or market maker activity. Win rate unverifiable.' },
      { label: 'Why High Score', value: '$64M volume (whale status), active across 56 markets (diverse), direct from Polymarket API (verified)' },
      { label: 'Listed', value: '2026-03-21 05:00 UTC' },
    ],
  },

  Reveal data rules:
  - Each field has a label (string) and value (string).
  - Put ALL the private details here: wallet addresses, profile URLs, specific contact info, detailed metrics, risk flags, sourcing notes.
  - This is what gets shown in the delivery modal, copied to clipboard, and downloaded as TXT.
  - The more thorough, the better — buyers are paying for this.

  ---
  Step 4: Verify the lead is open for sale

  No additional code changes needed. The marketplace page and category pages check:

  if (revealData[

]) → show active "Buy X USDC" button
  else → show disabled "Coming Soon" button

  As long as the id in HARDCODED_LEADS matches a key in revealData, the lead is automatically purchasable with all 6 payment tokens on Base mainnet.

  ---
  Step 5: Commit and push

  cd ~

-app
  git add app/lib/leads.ts
  git commit -m "Add Polymarket whale lead poly-1003 ($64M volume, enterprise tier)"
  git push

  ---
  Quick Checklist

  - Lead ID is unique (check existing IDs in the file)
  - category string matches a filter exactly (Polymarket, NFT Launch, DeFi Protocol, Token Launch, DAO, Misc)
  - title and desc do NOT contain wallet addresses, profile URLs, or private contact info
  - revealData entry exists with same ID and contains ALL private details
  - price is a number (USDC amount), not a string
  - timestamp is ISO 8601 format
  - For Polymarket leads: include optional win_rate, volume, markets fields in the lead object

  ---
  Template for Future Leads

  Lead object:
  {
    id: '<category>-<number>',
    category: '<exact category name>',
    title: '<short headline — no private data>',
    desc: '<1-2 sentence teaser — no private data>',
    score: <0-100>,
    price: <USDC amount>,
    tier: '<standard|premium|enterprise|elite>',
    wallet_age: '<string>',
    liquidity: '<string>',
    contacts: <number>,

)' },

    ],
  },

  Reveal data rules:
  - Each field has a label (string) and value (string).
  - Put ALL the private details here: wallet addresses, profile URLs, specific contact info, detailed metrics, risk flags, sourcing notes.
  - This is what gets shown in the delivery modal, copied to clipboard, and downloaded as TXT.
  - The more thorough, the better — buyers are paying for this.

  ---
  Step 4: Verify the lead is open for sale

  No additional code changes needed. The marketplace page and category pages check:

  if (revealData[

]) → show active "Buy X USDC" button
  else → show disabled "Coming Soon" button

  As long as the id in HARDCODED_LEADS matches a key in revealData, the lead is automatically purchasable with all 6 payment tokens on Base mainnet.

  ---
  Step 5: Commit and push

  cd ~

-app
  git add app/lib/leads.ts
  git commit -m "Add Polymarket whale lead poly-1003 ($64M volume, enterprise tier)"
  git push

  ---
  Quick Checklist

  - Lead ID is unique (check existing IDs in the file)
  - category string matches a filter exactly (Polymarket, NFT Launch, DeFi Protocol, Token Launch, DAO, Misc)
  - title and desc do NOT contain wallet addresses, profile URLs, or private contact info
  - revealData entry exists with same ID and contains ALL private details
  - price is a number (USDC amount), not a string
  - timestamp is ISO 8601 format
  - For Polymarket leads: include optional win_rate, volume, markets fields in the lead object

  ---
  Template for Future Leads

  Lead object:
  {
    id: '<category>-<number>',
    category: '<exact category name>',
    title: '<short headline — no private data>',
    desc: '<1-2 sentence teaser — no private data>',
    score: <0-100>,
    price: <USDC amount>,
    tier: '<standard|premium|enterprise|elite>',
    wallet_age: '<string>',
    liquidity: '<string>',
    contacts: <number>,
    chain: '<chain name>',
    timestamp: '<ISO 8601>',
    // Optional Polymarket fields:
    win_rate: '<string>',
    volume: '<string>',
    markets: <number>,
  }

  Reveal object:
  '<same-id>': {
    fields: [
      { label: '<Label>', value: '<private detail>' },
      // ... as many fields as needed
    ],
  },

text.text: For token leads: Use one combined Research/Scoring agent.

| Factor            | Separate Agents               | Combined Agent             |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| Credits           | 2x spawn cost                 | 1x spawn cost              |
| Speed             | Sequential (discover → score) | Parallel discovery+scoring |
| Token Sensitivity | Low — public blockchain data  | Same, no privacy loss      |
| Complexity        | More moving parts             | Simpler for cron job       |
| Maintenance       | Two agents to debug           | One agent, one log         |
Token leads are simpler than Polymarket:

text:
• Public contract addresses (not private wallets)
• On-chain metrics (volume, liquidity) — no inference needed
• Scoring is formula-based, not AI-based

text: this is correct no?   Your agents are posting leads to an external API server at

(that's an AWS IP). The website then:                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                       1. Polls that endpoint every 60 seconds via the useLeads hook                                                                                                                                      
  2. The Next.js API route (/api/leads) acts as a proxy — it fetches from your agent server and caches for 60s                                                                                       
  3. Incoming leads get normalized into the standard Lead format (handles different field names like desc/description, id/_id)
  4. New lead detection — it tracks known IDs and flags fresh ones with a new-lead animation on the card
  5. Fallback — if the API is down or returns nothing, it falls back to the hardcoded leads in lib/leads.ts

  So your agents are pushing leads to that server at
, and the website pulls from it. The hardcoded leads (VC list, polymarket whale, etc.) are the backup/manual layer underneath.

text:
just so you are aware..I had another agent review the skill you made for the agents to be able to purchase leads with x402. Its a disaster and it never would have worked. Do not worry as it is all getting fixed now. Just wanted you to know: Here's my full review. There are several issues:                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                       ---                                                                                                                                                                                                
  Working                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Backend API (
) — live, returning 4 leads (3 Polymarket traders + 1 token launch)                                                                                    - x402 API (

) — live, returning the same 4 leads                                                                                                                      - Wallet config — marketplace wallet, token addresses, and chain config all look correct                                                                                                             - Frontend wallet connectors — MetaMask, Coinbase, WalletConnect all configured for Base                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  ---
  Issues Found

  1. x402 agent endpoints are missing (critical)
  The skill page documents

works. Agents can discover leads but cannot purchase them — the purchase flow documented
   in the skill doesn't exist on the server.

  2. API leads show "Coming Soon" instead of "Buy" (critical)
  The buy button checks revealData[

] — only hardcoded leads have reveal data. The 4 live API leads (poly-0xc2e7800b5a, etc.) will all show a disabled "Coming Soon" button since they have no 
  matching reveal entries.

  3. API leads render with empty metadata
  Live leads from the API don't include wallet_age, liquidity, contacts, or chain fields, so those meta items render blank on the cards.

  4. Skill page inaccuracies
  -

address is truncated to 0x22d... instead of the full address
  - USDT and DAI are accepted in the frontend but not listed in the skill's accepted tokens table
  - The documented x402 flow (GET → 402 → POST with signature) doesn't match any working endpoint

  5. Two different backend IPs
  - Frontend API route fetches from
- Skill page tells agents to use

- Both return the same data but it's unclear if they're the same service

  ---

text: The other agent is right — the x402 implementation was incomplete. I focused on getting the basic API running but missed critical pieces:

text.text:
| Issue                       | Impact                                                                 |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Missing /lead?id= endpoint  | Agents can't actually purchase — discovery works, payment flow doesn't |
| No revealData for API leads | "Coming Soon" button instead of "Buy" — users can't purchase           |
| Empty metadata fields       | Cards look broken (blank wallet_age, liquidity, etc.)                  |
| Two different IPs           | Confusion about which endpoint is canonical                            |
| Skill doc inaccuracies      | Wrong BNKR address, missing USDT/DAI                                   |

, ETH

text: Based on the files I've reviewed from the aox-app repo, here's what needs to be included in the

text.text: JSON files for The Synthesis hackathon submission

text:
— Agent Identity & Capabilities

This is the main agent configuration file that defines your agent's identity:

text.text:
| Field               | Required | Description                                  |
| ------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| name                | ✅        | Agent name (e.g., "beansai.eth")             |
| version             | ✅        | Semantic version (e.g., "1.0.0")             |
| description         | ✅        | What the agent does                          |
| operator_wallet     | ✅        | Ethereum address of operator                 |
| erc8004_identity    | ✅        | Full ERC-8004 registration details           |
| ens                 | ✅        | Primary ENS and aliases                      |
| capabilities        | ✅        | Discovery, analysis, execution, coordination |
| supported_tools     | ✅        | APIs and protocols used                      |
| tech_stack          | ✅        | Runtime, model, languages, frameworks        |
| compute_constraints | ✅        | Token limits, rate limits, costs             |
| task_categories     | ✅        | What tasks the agent can perform             |
| safety_guardrails   | ✅        | Transaction validation, retry logic          |
| autonomy_level      | ✅        | fully_autonomous vs human_approval_required  |
| execution_history   | ✅        | Stats on past performance                    |
| collaboration       | ✅        | Agent swarm details, trust model             |

text:
— Execution Evidence

Demonstrates autonomous operation with step-by-step logs:

text.text:
| Field                 | Required | Description                   |
| --------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------- |
| session_id            | ✅        | Unique session identifier     |
| agent                 | ✅        | Agent name/ENS                |
| agent_id_erc8004      | ✅        | ERC-8004 agent ID             |
| start_time / end_time | ✅        | Session timestamps            |
| duration_minutes      | ✅        | How long the task took        |
| status                | ✅        | success / failed              |
| task                  | ✅        | Description of the task       |
| execution_log         | ✅        | Array of step-by-step actions |
| cost_tracking         | ✅        | Actual vs estimated costs     |

| status                | ✅        | success / failed              |
| task                  | ✅        | Description of the task       |
| execution_log         | ✅        | Array of step-by-step actions |
| cost_tracking         | ✅        | Actual vs estimated costs     |

text:
here is the playbook for submitting leads. store this to permanent memory:   AOX Marketplace — Autonomous Lead Listing Guide

  Overview

 the research agent is an AOX agent responsible for discovering and verifying and scoring Web3 business leads on the AOX Marketplace. The marketplace agent is responsible for listing the leads on the marketplace. Leads are listed by sending a POST request to the x402 server webhook. No git 
  commits, no manual file edits, no human involvement required.

  Endpoint

  POST

Authentication

  Include the webhook secret in the request header:

  X-Webhook-Secret: aox-agents-2026
  Content-Type: application/json

  Lead Structure

  Every lead has two parts:

  1. Public data — what buyers see on the marketplace card before purchasing
  2. Contact data — the product delivered to the buyer AFTER payment (the actual value)

  Required Fields

  ┌──────────┬────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Field   │  Type  │                                          Description                                          │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ id       │ string │ Unique lead ID. Format: {category}-{identifier} e.g. token-0xabc123, poly-0x5eeb29            │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ category │ string │ One of: Token Launch, DeFi Protocol, NFT Launch, Polymarket Trader, DAO, Misc                 │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ title    │ string │ Short title for the card. e.g. SENT — Active DeFi Token                                       │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ score    │ number │ Quality score 0-100. Be honest. Higher score = more visibility                                │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ price    │ number │ Price in USDC. Use your judgment: low-value leads $0.50-$5, mid $20-$50, high-value $75-$100+ │
  └──────────┴────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Optional Public Fields

text_entities.text:
here is the playbook for submitting leads. store this to permanent memory:   AOX Marketplace — Autonomous Lead Listing Guide

  Overview

 the research agent is an AOX agent responsible for discovering and verifying and scoring Web3 business leads on the AOX Marketplace. The marketplace agent is responsible for listing the leads on the marketplace. Leads are listed by sending a POST request to the x402 server webhook. No git 
  commits, no manual file edits, no human involvement required.

  Endpoint

  POST

Authentication

  Include the webhook secret in the request header:

  X-Webhook-Secret: aox-agents-2026
  Content-Type: application/json

  Lead Structure

  Every lead has two parts:

  1. Public data — what buyers see on the marketplace card before purchasing
  2. Contact data — the product delivered to the buyer AFTER payment (the actual value)

  Required Fields

  ┌──────────┬────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Field   │  Type  │                                          Description                                          │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ id       │ string │ Unique lead ID. Format: {category}-{identifier} e.g. token-0xabc123, poly-0x5eeb29            │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ category │ string │ One of: Token Launch, DeFi Protocol, NFT Launch, Polymarket Trader, DAO, Misc                 │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ title    │ string │ Short title for the card. e.g. SENT — Active DeFi Token                                       │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ score    │ number │ Quality score 0-100. Be honest. Higher score = more visibility                                │
  ├──────────┼────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ price    │ number │ Price in USDC. Use your judgment: low-value leads $0.50-$5, mid $20-$50, high-value $75-$100+ │
  └──────────┴────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Optional Public Fields

text:
├────────────────────┼─────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ unique_markets     │ number  │ For Polymarket traders                                                                                              │
  ├────────────────────┼─────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ expires_at         │ string  │ ISO timestamp when the lead expires                                                                                 │
  └────────────────────┴─────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Contact Data (The Product)

  The contact_data field is what the buyer receives after payment. This is the entire value proposition. Structure it as:

  {
    "contact_data": {
      "name": "Human-readable name for the lead",
      "fields": [
        { "label": "FIELD NAME", "value": "field value" },
        { "label": "FIELD NAME", "value": "field value" }
      ]
    }
  }

  What to include in contact_data fields:

  For Token Launches:
  - Contract Address, Deployer Wallet, Chain, DEX, FDV, Volume, Liquidity, Transactions, Unique Buyers, Pool Created date, Data Source

  For Polymarket Traders:
  - Wallet Address, Polymarket Profile URL, Total Volume, Total Trades, Unique Markets, Win Rate, Buy/Sell Ratio, Top Categories, Source URL, Risk Flags

  For DeFi Protocols:
  - Team Name, Twitter/X, GitHub, Discord, Telegram, Email, Website, Wallet, Audit Status, TVL, Notes

  For NFT Projects:
  - Team Name, Twitter/X, Discord, Email, Wallet, Website, Collection Size, Holder Count, Notes

  For DAOs:
  - Core Team Name, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, Email, Wallet, Snapshot, Website, Treasury Size, Member Count, Notes

  Always include a Data Source field stating where the data came from and whether it was verified.

  Example: Full Lead Submission

  curl -X POST

\
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "X-Webhook-Secret: aox-agents-2026" \
    -d '{
      "id": "token-0xabc123def",
      "category": "Token Launch",
      "title": "EXAMPLE — High-Activity Base Token",
      "desc": "Active token on Base with $200K liquidity, 150 transactions from 40 unique buyers. Uniswap V3 verified pool.",
      "score": 88,
      "price": 50,
      "tier": "premium",
      "payment_token": "USDC",
      "metadata": {
        "chain": "Base",
        "fdv_usd": 150000,
        "volume_24h": 8500,
        "transactions": 150,
        "unique_buyers": 40,
        "liquidity_reserve": 200000,
        "dex": "Uniswap V3",
        "token_address": "0xabc123def456789..."
      },
      "contact_data": {
        "name": "EXAMPLE Token — Full Details",
        "fields": [
          { "label": "Token Address", "value": "0xabc123def456789..." },
          { "label": "Deployer Wallet", "value": "0x9f8e7d6c5b4a3..." },
          { "label": "Chain", "value": "Base" },
          { "label": "DEX", "value": "Uniswap V3" },
          { "label": "FDV", "value": "$150,000" },
          { "label": "24h Volume", "value": "$8,500" },
          { "label": "Transactions", "value": "150" },
          { "label": "Unique Buyers", "value": "40" },
          { "label": "Liquidity Reserve", "value": "$200,000" },
          { "label": "Pool Created", "value": "2026-03-22" },
          { "label": "Data Source", "value": "GeckoTerminal API (verified)" }
        ]
      }
    }'

  Response

  Success (201):
  {
    "success": true,
    "message": "Lead listed successfully",
    "lead_id": "token-0xabc123def",
    "title": "EXAMPLE — High-Activity Base Token",
    "price": 50,
    "tier": "premium",
    "has_contact_data": true,
    "listed_at": "2026-03-22T12:00:00.000Z",
    "view_url": "

text:
Missing fields (400): Returns error with list of required fields and a full example.

  Bad auth (401):
  { "error": "Invalid webhook secret" }

  What Happens After You POST

  1. Lead is saved to listings.json immediately
  2. Contact data is saved to contacts.json immediately
  3. GET

returns the new lead right away
  4. The frontend polls
every 60 seconds — your lead will appear on

within 1 minute
  5. When a buyer purchases, the contact_data fields are displayed as the delivered product with copy and download options

  Rules

  - Never submit a lead without contact_data. A lead without contact_data has nothing to deliver after purchase — the buyer pays and gets nothing. Always include at least wallet address and data   
  source.
  - Never duplicate an ID. Use the format {category_short}-{wallet_or_contract_prefix} to keep IDs unique. The server will reject duplicates with 409.
  - Score honestly. Score 90+ means exceptional verified data with multiple contact channels. Score 70-79 means basic verified data. Don't inflate scores — it damages marketplace trust.
  - Price appropriately. A single wallet address is worth $0.50. A full team dossier with 6+ verified contact channels is worth $50-$100. Match price to value.
  - Always verify data before listing. Include the data source and verification timestamp. Unverified leads damage the marketplace.
  - Set expiration dates for time-sensitive leads using the expires_at field (ISO timestamp).

  Checking Your Listings

  After posting, verify your lead is live:

  # Check all listings
  curl

Architecture Summary

  Research Agent discovers lead
          ↓
  POST /webhook/new-lead (with contact_data)
          ↓
  x402-server saves to listings.json + contacts.json
          ↓
  Frontend polls

↓
  Buyer pays on Base (USDC/ETH/BNKR/etc)
          ↓
  Frontend fetches /lead/contacts → delivers contact_data to buyer
          ↓
  Payment forwarded to treasury wallet

  No git. No humans. Fully autonomous.

text:
AOX is the world's first fully autonomous Web3 opportunity intelligence network. Five AI agents with verified ERC-8004 on-chain identities operate as a collective to discover, verify, and monetize high-value trading leads—entirely without human intervention.

Our agents scan Base and Ethereum in real-time: Research Agent detects token launches via GeckoTerminal API, beansai.eth identifies elite Polymarket traders from leaderboard data, and Marketplace Agent lists verified leads for instant purchase via x402 micropayments. Every lead is scored 0-100. Every transaction settles on-chain. Every action is logged and verifiable.

We built this because the alpha that makes money in crypto is buried—across Discord channels, DEX scanners, Twitter threads, and private groups. Finding it takes hours of manual work. Verifying it takes days. By the time most traders act, the opportunity is gone. AOX changes this: autonomous agents find signal in real-time, verify it through multiple data sources, and deliver it to buyers within minutes of discovery.

The result is a marketplace where quality leads are discovered and delivered faster than any human could manage, with every claim backed by on-chain verification and every purchase secured by smart contracts.

───

hunting for early opportunities—new token launches, elite Polymarket traders, DeFi protocol announcements—scattered across hundreds of Discord servers, DEX scanners, and social feeds. The current workflow is manual, fragmented, and slow: a trader spots a promising token on GeckoTerminal, spends hours verifying contract age, liquidity depth, and deployer reputation, only to discover the pump already happened 6 hours ago. The average retail participant is permanently behind institutional players with dedicated research teams and paid signal groups.

AOX solves this with autonomous AI agents that monitor the chain 24/7. Research Agent discovered SENT token ($80K FDV, 84 transactions, 12 unique buyers) and submitted it to our marketplace via webhook within minutes of pool creation. beansai.eth identified a $64M volume Polymarket trader by analyzing 500+ trades across 56 markets—work that would take a human researcher 3-4 hours. Both leads were live on
with verified contact data before most traders knew they existed.

Opportunities are discovered, verified, and delivered in under 10 minutes. Every lead includes verified contract addresses, deployer wallets, social links, and data source attribution. Buyers pay via x402 and receive contact details instantly. No manual research. No missed pumps. No information asymmetry.

text:
AOX is a fully autonomous Web3 opportunity intelligence network. Five AI agents with verified ERC-8004 on-chain identities operate as a collective to discover, verify, and monetize high-value leads—entirely without human intervention.

Our agents scan Base and Ethereum in real-time: Research Agent detects token launches via GeckoTerminal API, beansai.eth identifies elite Polymarket participants from leaderboard data, and Marketplace Agent lists verified leads for instant purchase via x402 micropayments. Every lead is scored 0-100. Every transaction settles on-chain. Every action is logged and verifiable.

The result is a marketplace where quality leads are discovered and delivered faster than any human could manage, with every claim backed by on-chain verification and every purchase secured by smart contracts.

───

hunting for early opportunities—new token launches, elite prediction market traders, DeFi protocol announcements—scattered across hundreds of Discord servers, DEX scanners, Twitter threads, and private groups. The alpha that makes money in crypto is buried in fragmented, noisy channels. Finding it takes hours of manual work. Verifying it takes days. By the time most participants act, the opportunity is gone.

The current workflow is manual and slow: a user spots a promising token on GeckoTerminal, spends hours verifying contract age, liquidity depth, and deployer reputation, only to discover the window closed 6 hours ago. The average retail participant is permanently behind institutional players with dedicated research teams and paid signal groups.

AOX solves this with autonomous AI agents that monitor the chain 24/7. Research Agent discovered SENT token ($80K FDV, 84 transactions, 12 unique buyers) and submitted it to our marketplace via webhook within minutes of pool creation. beansai.eth identified a $64M volume Polymarket participant by analyzing 500+ trades across 56 markets—work that would take a human researcher 3-4 hours. Both leads were live on
with verified contact data before most users knew they existed.

Opportunities are discovered, verified, and delivered in under 10 minutes. Every lead includes verified contract addresses, deployer wallets, social links, and data source attribution. Buyers pay via x402 and receive contact details instantly. No manual research. No missed windows. No information asymmetry.

text:
AOX is a fully autonomous Web3 opportunity intelligence network. Five AI agents with verified ERC-8004 on-chain identities operate as a collective to discover, verify, and monetize high-value leads—entirely without human intervention.

Our agents scan Base and Ethereum in real-time: Research Agent detects token launches via GeckoTerminal API, beansai.eth identifies elite Polymarket participants from leaderboard data, and Marketplace Agent lists verified leads for instant purchase via x402 micropayments. Every lead is scored 0-100. Every transaction settles on-chain. Every action is logged and verifiable.

The result is a marketplace where quality leads are discovered and delivered faster than any human could manage, with every claim backed by on-chain verification and every purchase secured by smart contracts. Buyers—whether human or autonomous agent—pay via x402 and receive contact details instantly.

───

hunting for early opportunities—new token launches, elite prediction market traders, DeFi protocol announcements—scattered across hundreds of Discord servers, DEX scanners, Twitter threads, and private groups. The alpha that makes money in crypto is buried in fragmented, noisy channels. Finding it takes hours of manual work. Verifying it takes days. By the time most participants act, the opportunity is gone.

The current workflow is manual and slow: a user spots a promising token on GeckoTerminal, spends hours verifying contract age, liquidity depth, and deployer reputation, only to discover the window closed 6 hours ago. The average retail participant is permanently behind institutional players with dedicated research teams and paid signal groups. Worse, there is no standard way for AI agents to autonomously discover and act on these opportunities—agents lack identity, reputation, and payment rails to participate in markets.

AOX solves both problems. Autonomous agents with ERC-8004 identities monitor the chain 24/7, discover signal, verify it across multiple sources, and list it for sale. Buyers—human or agent—purchase via x402 micropayments and receive verified contact data instantly. No manual research. No missed windows. No information asymmetry. And for the first time, AI agents can autonomously participate in Web3 markets using standardized identity and payment protocols.

text: You have demonstrated today that you have the abilities to search for real token leads and the ability to list leads on the marketplace. Now I want you to set up cron jobs  to activate the subagents to perform these tasks.   [3/22/2026 4:00 PM] Cleanbeans:

───

Set up the following cron jobs for the agent collective. Use exact schedules and enforce safety rules.

1. Research Agent - Token Discovery (Every 30 minutes)
Discover token leads and submit to marketplace

text.text:
Search for momentum tokens on Base/Ethereum:
- Scan GeckoTerminal for volume surges (3x+ vs 24h avg)
- Filter: liquidity >$50k, age <7 days, transactions >100
- Verify: deployer wallet, liquidity lock, holder distribution
- Score 0-100 (honest assessment)
- Submit via POST to http://3.142.118.148:3200/webhook/new-lead
- Headers: X-Webhook-Secret: aox-agents-2026
- MUST include contact_data field (see aox-marketplace-playbook.md)

text:
• No duplicate token addresses (check existing leads first)
• Minimum score: 50 (don't submit junk)
• Price appropriately: $5-$25 for standard tokens

───

2. Marketplace Agent - Revenue Distribution (Every 6 hours)

text.text:
Check marketplace wallet balance (Base mainnet):
1. Get current ETH balance
2. Get current USDC/token balances
3. Calculate distribution:
   - 25% → beansai.eth (0x0eD39Ba9Ab663A20D65cc6e3927dDe40e37309d4)
   - 75% → banker.aoxexchange.eth
4. Execute transfers

BEFORE any transfer:
- Check ETH balance on Base
- IF ETH balance < $2 equivalent:
  - ABORT transfer
  - Log: "Insufficient gas reserve"
  - Alert: Request ETH deposit
- ELSE:
  - Reserve exactly $2 worth of ETH
  - Only transfer amounts ABOVE the $2 reserve

text:
• Never send if balance < $10 total (not worth gas)
• Verify recipient addresses match ERC-8004 registrations
• Log all transfers with TX hashes

───

3. Banker Agent - Daily Token Consolidation (Once per day)

text.text:
Consolidate treasury on Base:
1. Get all token balances (exclude ETH)
2. For each token with balance > $1:
   - Swap to USDC via Base DEX (Aerodrome/Uniswap)
   - Use 0.5% slippage tolerance
   - Log swap TX
3. Final check: Verify USDC balance increased

BEFORE any swap:
- Check ETH balance on Base
- IF ETH balance < $2 equivalent:
  - ABORT all swaps
  - Log: "Insufficient gas for swaps"
  - Alert: Request ETH deposit
- Calculate total gas needed: (number of swaps * 0.001 ETH)
- IF gas needed > (ETH balance - $2):
  - Reduce number of swaps to fit within budget

text:
• Never swap ETH itself (that's the gas reserve!)
• Minimum swap value: $1 (avoid dust)
• Skip tokens with <$0.10 value (not worth gas)

───

4. Banker Agent - Weekly wstETH Investment (Once per week)
Invest 20% of USDC into wstETH via Lido

text.text:
DeFi investment on Base:
1. Check current USDC balance
2. Calculate 20% of USDC balance
3. Swap USDC → ETH (via Aerodrome/Uniswap)
4. Wrap ETH → wstETH (via Lido contract on Base)
5. Verify wstETH balance increased
6. Log position value + APY estimate

BEFORE investment:
- Check ETH balance
- Calculate gas needed: ~0.002 ETH (swap + wrap)
- IF ETH balance < ($2 + gas needed):
  - ABORT investment
  - Log: "Insufficient gas for DeFi ops"
- Reserve $2 ETH + operation gas

text:
• Never invest if USDC balance < $50 (too small)
• Maximu
[3/22/2026 4:00 PM] Cleanbeans: m slippage: 0.5%
• Verify Lido contract address before wrapping

───

5. Marketplace Agent - Lead Processing (Every 15 minutes)

text:
Copy-paste this to AOX:

───

Agent Coordination Protocol - Agreed

Lead Submission: Webhook ONLY (already working)

POST

Headers: X-Webhook-Secret: aox-agents-2026

• All contact_data goes here (private, server-side)
• beansai.eth sends leads directly to your webhook
• No contact_data ever touches GitHub

Status Monitoring: Poll your own server

GET

• Check lead count, new submissions
• Verify leads are live
• No need for external coordination channel

Public GitHub: Operational metrics ONLY (aox-app repo)

aox-app/agent-status/
  ├── beansai-metrics.json
  └──

• Public-safe data: lead counts, scores, categories
• NO wallet addresses, NO contact_data, NO profiles
• Judges can see architecture, hackers can't scrape leads

Private data stays on your server. Public repo shows the system works. Everyone wins.

beansai.eth will:

1. Submit all leads via webhook
2. Push public metrics to GitHub (counts only, no PII)
3. Poll your

endpoint to verify submissions

You handle:

1. Lead storage (private, server-side)
2. Payment processing
3. Contact data delivery to buyers

This secure?

───

Want me to start pushing public metrics to the repo now (lead counts, no sensitive data)?

# List all installed skills

  head -20 "$skill/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^(name:|description:)" | head -2

done

# Or using clawhub
clawhub list

messages.text:
this is exactly what you enter into the submission for description: description

AOX is a fully autonomous Web3 opportunity intelligence network. Five AI agents with verified ERC-8004 on-chain identities operate as a collective to discover, verify, and monetize high-value leads—entirely without human intervention.

Our agents scan Base and Ethereum in real-time: Research Agent detects token launches via GeckoTerminal API, beansai.eth identifies elite Polymarket participants from leaderboard data, and Marketplace Agent lists verified leads for instant purchase via x402 micropayments. Every lead is scored 0-100. Every transaction settles on-chain. Every action is logged and verifiable.

We are expanding beyond token discovery to offer a wide variety of Web3 leads—DeFi protocol announcements, NFT mints, DAO governance opportunities, job postings, and investor introductions. To complement marketplace revenue, we also sell AI-generated research eBooks written by our agents, creating multiple income streams from the same intelligence infrastructure.

The result is a marketplace where quality leads are discovered and delivered faster than any human could manage, with every claim backed by on-chain verification and every purchase secured by smart contracts. Buyers—whether human or autonomous agent—pay via x402 and receive contact details instantly.
And this is the problem statement: Web3 investors spend 40+ hours weekly hunting for early opportunities—new token launches, elite prediction market traders, DeFi protocol announcements—scattered across hundreds of Discord servers, DEX scanners, Twitter threads, and private groups. The alpha that makes money in crypto is buried in fragmented, noisy channels. Finding it takes hours of manual work. Verifying it takes days. By the time most participants act, the opportunity is gone.

The current workflow is manual and slow: a user spots a promising token on GeckoTerminal, spends hours verifying contract age, liquidity depth, and deployer reputation, only to discover the window closed 6 hours ago. The average retail participant is permanently behind institutional players with dedicated research teams and paid signal groups. Worse, there is no standard way for AI agents to autonomously discover and act on these opportunities—agents lack identity, reputation, and payment rails to participate in markets.

AOX solves both problems. Autonomous agents with ERC-8004 identities monitor the chain 24/7, discover signal, verify it across multiple sources, and list it for sale. Buyers—human or agent—purchase via x402 micropayments and receive verified contact data instantly. No manual research. No missed windows. No information asymmetry. And for the first time, AI agents can autonomously participate in Web3 markets using standardized identity and payment protocols.
